title: Festive German Roasted Tomatoes Stir-fry
description: This herbal and festive German dish features roasted tomatoes combined with a dash of friendly flavors for a light and savory side or appetizer.

ingredients.name: Tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Use any variety of tomatoes, such as Roma or cherry

ingredients.name: Extra-virgin Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: Can be replaced with avocado oil for a heartier taste

ingredients.name: Garlic Clove
ingredients.notes: Sliced into thin rounds or minced finely to enhance the flavor

ingredients.name: Red Pepper Flakes
ingredients.notes: For a bit of heat and depth, add 1/4-1/2 tsp based on preference

ingredients.name: Fresh Herbs
ingredients.notes: Use either for variety or both to complement the tomatoes well

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: To taste, adjust based on the saltiness of your tomato varieties and flavors used

ingredients.name: Black Pepper
ingredients.notes: Can be omitted or replaced with any mild seasoning you prefer for a milder dish

instructions: Wash and dry tomatoes. Cut in half.
Place tomato halves on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, cut sides up.

In a small bowl, mix olive oil, minced garlic, red pepper flakes (if using), salt, and black pepper to create the rub. Apply this mixture generously over the tomato halves, ensuring they are evenly coated.
Roast tomatoes in preheated oven for 20-25 minutes until golden brown and slightly caramelized on the edges.
While tomatoes are roasting, chop fresh herbs finely or leave them whole if using dill as a garnish.
After tomatoes come out of the oven, stir them gently to redistribute any juices that may have pooled at the bottom. Remove from heat.
Combine roasted tomatoes with chopped fresh herbs in a serving bowl and mix thoroughly until all ingredients are evenly distributed.
Garnish with additional fresh herbs if desired before serving.
